title: Dendranthema lavandulifolium
taxonomy: Dendranthema lavandulifolium is classified under the family Asteraceae. This classification reflects its membership in the large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as daisies. The genus Dendranthema is a part of the tribe Anthemideae, which consists of various species of daisies. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Dendranthema lavandulifolium has characteristics such as composite flowers and a taproot system. Its classification within the Asteraceae family also indicates its evolutionary relationships with other plants in this group.

intro: Dendranthema lavandulifolium, also known as Lavender-leaved Chrysanthemum, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Dendranthema lavandulifolium include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are among the most common herbal preparations made with Dendranthema lavandulifolium.
